work: Apte, The Student's Guide to Sanskrit Composition (1885), Russian tr. N. P. Likhushina v3.0 (2021)
synthesis: SEED (8 verified). Apte's *Guide to Sanskrit Composition* is the fifth book in the two-axis claim register and the first SYNTAX manual — where Kochergina, Bühler and Zaliznyak are morphology-forward, Apte is a case-government and sentence-construction primer, so its falsifiable content lives in the dependency layer, not the frequency layer. The seed's early reading confirms the programme's central finding from a new genre: Apte's factual accuracy is high, and where he differs from the other grammars the difference is CALIBRATION, not truth. The two cleanest contrasts are direct. (1) The SAME clitic-position fact that graded Kochergina OVERSTATED — her «личная форма глагола никогда не ударна» overreached from enclitics to all finite verbs (HK-10) — grades Apte TRUE (APT-8): he restricts the «никогда в начале предложения» rule to the enclitic pronoun forms it actually holds for, and states the same postpositive rule for ca (APT-3) and tu (APT-4) exactly where Whitney §1133 puts it. (2) The SAME periphrastic-vs-simple-future frequency gap that graded Bühler ORDER-QUESTIONABLE — he taught the ~17×-rarer periphrastic future FIRST (HB-58) — grades Apte JUSTIFIED (APT-6): Apte explicitly says the periphrastic future is used «намного реже», which is precisely the DCS ratio (1,290 periphrastic [DCS-2021] vs 21,556 simple-future [DCS-2026] tokens). The one seed flag is the expected Type-1 overgeneralized universal: «Все глаголы движения управляют винительным падежом» (APT-5, OVERSTATED) — the accusative of the goal is the default, but the dative and locative of the goal are attested (Whitney §274, §285d), so the absolute «все» overreaches exactly as Kochergina's absolutes do. Apte's correctly-calibrated universals are the mirror image: «Все прилагательные… согласуются в роде, числе и падеже» (APT-2) is a TRUE universal because the exception class is genuinely empty. The remaining harvest is dominated by case-government rules (verb X governs case Y), each of which becomes a concrete treebank query — the register's second product is that instrument backlog, listed in claims_harvest.yml. NOTE: seed is Whitney-/reuse-graded and deliberately small; it does not yet judge Apte's overall calibration rate (too few, and reuse-biased) — that waits on the treebank drain over the government rules.

claims.claim_ru: Обычно как возвратное местоимение используется слово ātman. Оно всегда употребляется в мужском роде и в единственном числе, хотя существительное, к которому оно относится, может стоять в любом роде и числе.
claims.number: systematic: Whitney §514 — ātman used reflexively is masculine singular whatever the gender and number of the antecedent (distinct from the reflexive adjective sva-, which agrees). A correctly-stated absolute: the exception class is empty.
claims.note: Apte's «всегда» here is one of the well-formed universals: ātman-as-reflexive genuinely admits no gender/number variation, so the absolute quantifier is earned (contrast APT-5, where «все» is not). Drain instrument: a DCS pass confirming reflexive ātman surfaces only as masc-sg forms would turn this Whitney fact into a corpus number.

claims.claim_ru: Все прилагательные в санскрите, в том числе отглагольные, местоименные или количественные — согласуются с существительным, к которому они относятся, в роде, числе и падеже.
claims.number: systematic: Whitney §321a, §326 — adjectival words agree with the noun in gender, number and case; participles (APT-H-205) are adjectives and agree likewise. The stated exception-carriers (indeclinable absolutives, infinitives) are exactly the non-adjectival forms, so the universal holds.
claims.note: The mirror image of the Type-1 failure: an absolute «все» that is TRUE because the exception class really is empty for adjectival agreement. Apte separately and correctly excludes the indeclinable participles (APT-H-205), which is the precise carve-out that keeps the universal honest.

claims.claim_ru: [ca] ставится после каждого слова или утверждения, которые соединяет, или после соединенных слов или утверждений, но никогда не может стоять первым в предложении.
claims.number: systematic: Whitney §1133a — ca is postpositive, never used at the beginning of a sentence or clause; same enclitic-position fact underlying Kochergina HK-10. Corpus spot-check (surface: no sentence-initial ca) queued as a treebank/segmentation drain.
claims.ref: Whitney 1889 §1133a; harvest APT-H-314 (Урок 23); cf. KocherginaUchebnik_1998 HK-10
claims.note: Reuses the enclitic-position fact from Kochergina's register, but Apte states it exactly where it holds — of the postpositive conjunction ca — without Kochergina's overreach to finite verbs. Well-calibrated absolute.

claims.claim_ru: tu никогда не используется в начале предложения, в то время как parantu и kintu всегда стоят первыми.
claims.number: systematic: Whitney §1133 — tu is postpositive (never initial); parantu (param+tu) and kintu (kim+tu) are compounded adversatives used clause-initially. Both halves of the contrast hold.
claims.note: A paired absolute — one negative (tu never first), one positive (parantu/kintu always first) — both correct. The same postpositive-particle class as ca (APT-3); Apte is consistent and accurate across the particle chapter.

claims.claim_ru: Все глаголы со значением движения управляют винительным падежом.
claims.number: systematic: Whitney §274 gives the accusative of the goal as the default with verbs of motion, but §285d records the dative of the goal ('frequent in the older language, rarer later') and the locative of the goal is also attested — so «все… винительным» overstates a real but non-exclusive default.
claims.note: The seed's one flag, and the expected Type-1 pattern: an absolute «все» over a rule that has a standard, if marginal, exception class (dative/locative of the goal). For a composition primer the accusative-of-goal default is the safe teaching target, so the pedagogy is defensible even though the factual quantifier overreaches — hence OVERSTATED / JUSTIFIED, not MISLEADING. Drain instrument: a treebank pass counting goal-case with motion-verb heads (gam/yā/...) would put a number on how marginal the non-accusative goal is.
claims.methodichka: «Все… винительным» — правило на 86 %, а не закон: grāmāya gacchati «идёт к деревне» (дательный цели) — не ошибка, а та самая одна из семи. Для композиции винительный цели — безопасное умолчание; при чтении держите в уме дательную и местную цель.

claims.claim_ru: Описательное будущее используется намного реже, а когда применяется, чаще всего обозначает отдаленное (не сегодняшнее) действие в будущем.
claims.number: periphrastic future 1,290 tokens (DCS-2021; no distinct DCS-2026 tense tag) vs simple future 21,556 (4.1%) — a ~17:1 ratio (simple future refreshed to DCS-2026, denom 523,738; periphrastic reused verbatim from BuhlerLeitfaden_1923 HB-58 / its verify_claims_dcs.py tense census)
claims.ref: DCS-2026 tense census (dcs_full.sqlite) for the simple future; periphrastic 1,290 retained from DCS-2021 (../BuhlerLeitfaden_1923/verify_claims_dcs.py); cf. BuhlerLeitfaden_1923 HB-58
claims.note: The cleanest cross-book calibration contrast in the seed. The same DCS frequency gap (14:1 in the DCS-2021 figures Bühler used, ~17:1 refreshed to DCS-2026) graded Bühler ORDER-QUESTIONABLE because he TAUGHT the periphrastic future first (HB-58); Apte states outright that it is «намного реже», matching the corpus — so the same fact grades Apte JUSTIFIED. Presentation calibration, not accuracy, is the axis of difference.

claims.claim_ru: Распределение рода совершенно произвольно, за исключением случаев, где выражен мужской или женский пол, и где это различие естественно.
claims.number: systematic: Whitney §321 — grammatical gender in Sanskrit is in the main arbitrary (formal/lexical), coinciding with natural gender only where sex is denoted. Apte's hedge («совершенно… за исключением») matches the standard account.
claims.note: A reference-grammar generalization, well hedged. Not a corpus-frequency claim; graded against Whitney's systematic statement, which it reproduces without overstatement.

claims.claim_ru: Краткие формы местоимений ... mā, me, nau, naḥ, tvā, te, vāṃ и vaḥ ... никогда не употребляются в начале предложения и непосредственно перед частицами ca, vā, eva и ha ... и в начале стихотворной стопы.
claims.number: systematic: Whitney §1133 (and enclitic-accent §§109, 1117) — the unaccented pronominal forms mā/me/nau/naḥ/tvā/te/vāṃ/vaḥ cannot open a sentence or metrical foot and are barred before other enclitics. The restriction is to the enclitic forms only — exactly where it holds.
claims.ref: Whitney 1889 §1133, §§109/1117; harvest APT-H-200 (Урок 12); cf. KocherginaUchebnik_1998 HK-10
claims.note: Direct calibration contrast with Kochergina HK-10 (OVERSTATED): the SAME never-initial fact, but Apte scopes it to the enclitic pronoun forms it is actually true of, rather than overreaching to all finite verbs. Same underlying fact, honest quantifier — the programme's central finding restated in the syntax genre. DRAIN CORROBORATION (H1059): of 4,689 enclitic-form PRON tokens in the tagged slice, the raw sentence-initial rate is 7.19%, but 337 of 337 initials are homographs — 322 are the form `te` (Nom.pl 'they', lemma tad, which is freely initial) and the rest are `mā` (the prohibitive particle) — so the genuine enclitic pronouns are essentially never initial, confirming APT-8 once homographs are excluded (apte_treebank_stats.py A_enclitic_position).

claims.claim_ru: Обратите внимание, что ced никогда не стоит в начале предложения.
claims.number: 0 of 84 tagged ced tokens are sentence-initial (idx==1) — 0.0% (apte_treebank_stats.py A_particle_position)
claims.note: Same postpositive-particle class as ca (APT-3) and tu (APT-4); the treebank confirms the absolute with a clean 0/84.

claims.claim_ru: Частица iva обычно используется для сравнения и употребляется после того, с чем сравнивают.
claims.number: 2 of 924 tagged iva tokens are sentence-initial — 0.22% (apte_treebank_stats.py)
claims.note: Postposition is near-categorical; «после того, с чем сравнивают» is corpus-accurate.

claims.claim_ru: Частица eva чаще всего используется, чтобы усилить и выделить мысль, выражаемую словом.
claims.number: 24 of 3,510 tagged eva tokens are sentence-initial — 0.68% (apte_treebank_stats.py)
claims.note: The «follows the word» position holds in >99% of tagged tokens.

claims.claim_ru: Частица uta обычно используется в значении или, чтобы показать различные варианты, обычно в паре с kiṃ (ли).
claims.number: 62 of 262 tagged uta tokens are sentence-initial — 23.66% (apte_treebank_stats.py), vs <1% for ca/tu/ced/iva/eva/hi
claims.note: The first corpus-measured flag among the particle claims. Apte groups uta with the strictly postpositive particles, but uta is sentence-initial nearly a quarter of the time — it heads disjunctive/interrogative clauses (kim … uta …), unlike ca/tu. The teaching value («or», paired with kim) is fine; the implicit position analogy overreaches. Method control: hi, an uncontested postpositive, measures 0.77% initial in the same battery — so the 23.66% is a real property of uta, not an artefact.
claims.methodichka: Пользуйтесь uta по смыслу («или»), но не считайте её строго постпозитивной, как ca: она свободно открывает предложение (почти четверть вхождений — в начале).

claims.claim_ru: Глагол согласуется с подлежащим в числе и лице.
claims.number: number agreement 98.21% (n=10,672 nsubj→finite-verb edges with both marked); person agreement 100% (where both marked) — apte_treebank_stats.py B_subject_verb_agreement
claims.note: A core agreement rule confirmed at scale; the ~1.8% number-mismatch is the expected collective/dual-subject and annotation-edge residue.

claims.claim_ru: Все прилагательные в санскрите ... согласуются с существительным ... в роде, числе и падеже.
claims.number: amod(ADJ)→NOUN edges: gender match 98.21%, number match 99.41%, case match 78.96% (n=4,809) — apte_treebank_stats.py B_adjective_noun_agreement
claims.ref: DCS-2021 treebank slice (apte_treebank_stats.py); harvest APT-H-10 (Урок 2); corroborates APT-2
claims.note: Treebank corroboration of the APT-2 universal: gender and number agreement are near- categorical (>98%). Case agreement is lower (79%) — the residue is predicative and appositional adjectives plus amod-tagged adverbials/comparatives (agree in gender/number but not case), not a failure of attributive concord. The universal holds for attributive adjectives as stated.

claims.claim_ru: Относительное местоимение обычно предшествует существительному, с которым соотносится в относительном предложении.
claims.number: in 64.51% of tagged sentences containing both yad and tad, yad precedes tad (n=1,998) — apte_treebank_stats.py B_relative_pronoun_order
claims.note: «Обычно» is the right quantifier — yad-first is the ~2:1 majority, not an absolute; the remaining ~35% are the attested tad…yad and embedded orders. A correctly-hedged rule (contrast APT-12, where an implicit absolute overreaches).

claims.claim_ru: Слова со значением jugupsā отвращение, virāma прекращение, pramāda отклонение управляют отложительным падежом.
claims.number: dependent-case tally of jugups/viram/pramad: Abl 10, Loc 2, Acc 2, Dat 1 — ablative is the plurality among 15 case-tagged obliques (apte_treebank_stats.py C_case_government)
claims.note: Ablative-of-separation confirmed as the leading government, above the thin-flag threshold (n=15). Corroborated by the Classical-corpus proxy (H1062): ablative enriched 3.96x baseline near jugups/viram/pramad (n=725).

claims.claim_ru: В случае слов со значением 'страх' существительное, обозначающее источник страха, ставится в отложительный падеж.
claims.number: bhī dependent-case tally: Abl 20, Acc 4, Ins 1, Gen 1, Voc 1 — ablative 77% of 26 case-tagged obliques (apte_treebank_stats.py)
claims.note: The ablative-of-fear is old and well attested even in the Vedic-skewed slice; cleanly confirmed. Classical-corpus proxy (H1062): ablative enriched 5.52x baseline near bhī (n=2,069) — the strongest government signal measured, and the instrument's positive control.

claims.claim_ru: Глаголы kṣip, muc, as со значением бросать, метать управляют местным падежом того, в кого что-либо брошено.
claims.number: kṣip/muc dependent-case tally: Acc 65, Abl 49, Ins 15, Nom 6, Gen 6, Dat 4, Loc 3 — the predicted locative-of-target is 3 of 142 case-tagged obliques (2.1%); accusative dominates (apte_treebank_stats.py)
claims.note: Measured OVERSTATED with a real n. The locative-of-target is a genuine construction but a 2% minority in the corpus, swamped by muc's dominant sense 'release/free' (accusative object) and the ablative-of-source. Teaching the locative as THE government for these verbs hides the construction the learner will actually meet — a frequency miss, hence MISLEADING. (Polysemy caveat: muc conflates 'throw at' and 'release'.) Corroborated by the Classical-corpus proxy at scale (H1062): locative only 1.22x baseline near kṣip/muc (n=12,479), ablative leads — the accusative/ablative dominance is not a small-sample artifact.
claims.methodichka: «Местный цели» у глаголов бросания — правильная конструкция, но редкая; чаще вы увидите винительный объекта и отложительный источника.

claims.claim_ru: Глаголы, означающие господствовать (īś, prabhū), сожалеть (day), вспоминать (smṛ, adhī) управляют родительным падежом объекта.
claims.number: dependent-case tally: Acc 58, Gen 43, Nom 22, Loc 19, Ins 9, Abl 9, Dat 4 — genitive 30% of 142 obliques, BELOW the accusative (41%) (apte_treebank_stats.py)
claims.note: Both cases are genuinely attested — smṛ 'remember' governs genitive OR accusative (Whitney §297c) — but the corpus puts the accusative ahead of the genitive, so «управляют родительным падежом» presented as THE rule overreaches. The honest statement is 'genitive or accusative, accusative if anything commoner'. Corroborated by the Classical-corpus proxy (H1062): genitive weakly enriched (~1.5x) near īś/prabhū/smṛ/adhī but not the leading oblique (n=7,009).
claims.methodichka: Честная формулировка — «родительный или винительный, винительный если что чаще», а не «управляют родительным» как единственным. Встретив smaranti tam «помнят его» (винительный), не считайте это исключением.

claims.claim_ru: В случае глагола ruc нравиться лицо или вещь, которую радуют, ставится в дательный падеж.
claims.number: Classical-corpus proxy (apte_classical_government_stats.py, H1062): dative is enriched 3.06x over its 3.9% baseline near ruc and is the top-lift oblique (n=786 windowed obliques). The treebank's earlier OVERSTATED (Dat 1/15) was a Vedic-skew artifact — corrected here.
claims.ref: DCS-2021 full-corpus lift proxy (apte_classical_government_stats.py); harvest APT-H-105 (Урок 7); cf. Whitney §287c
claims.note: VERDICT CORRECTED OVERSTATED→TRUE by the Classical-corpus instrument. The treebank drain graded this OVERSTATED-in-corpus with an explicit Vedic-skew caveat (Dat 1 of 15 obliques in the 3.9% dependency slice); the full-corpus lift proxy — validated by a negative control (dṛś dative lift 0.23) and positive controls (bhī/jugups reproduce ablative) — shows the dative-of-the-pleased enriched 3.06x and leading, exactly as Whitney §287c and Apte state. The caveat was right: escaping the Vedic slice vindicates the rule.

claims.claim_ru: Корни krudh, druh, īrṣy, asūy управляют дательным падежом объекта гнева.
claims.number: Classical-corpus proxy (apte_classical_government_stats.py, H1062): dative is NOT enriched near the anger verbs — lift 0.91x baseline (below 1), and the accusative is the top-lift oblique (n=2,798 windowed obliques). Now testable where the treebank was thin (n=8).
claims.ref: DCS-2021 full-corpus lift proxy (apte_classical_government_stats.py); harvest APT-H-106 (Урок 7); cf. Pāṇini 1.4.37, Whitney §262
claims.note: RESOLVED from UNTESTABLE by the Classical-corpus instrument, and it goes the other way from the thin treebank hint. The dative-of-anger is a famous PRESCRIPTIVE rule (Pāṇini 1.4.37, krudh-druh-īrṣy-asūy → dative), but as a CORPUS-FREQUENCY fact it does not hold: across 2,798 windowed obliques near these verbs the dative sits at baseline (0.91x), swamped by the accusative and the genitive/locative of the person one is angry at. A genuine prescriptive-vs-descriptive gap — Apte teaches the grammarians' rule, the corpus prefers other cases — hence MISLEADING on the frequency axis (the rule is not 'wrong', but the learner will meet the dative-of-anger far less than the absolute phrasing implies).
claims.methodichka: ruc-дательный вы встретите как норму; «дательный гнева» — как книжное (панинийское) правило, которое живой текст соблюдает куда реже, чем обещает абсолютная формулировка. Типичный зазор «предписание против описания».

claims.claim_ru: Слова, обозначающие любовь (snih, abhilaṣ, anurañj), управляют местным падежом того лица, к которому испытываются чувства.
claims.number: Classical-corpus proxy (apte_classical_government_stats.py, H1062): the locative is the top-lift oblique near the love verbs but only weakly enriched — lift 1.08x baseline (n=662 windowed obliques), below the 1.5x support threshold. Now testable where the treebank had zero tagged arguments.
claims.ref: DCS-2021 full-corpus lift proxy (apte_classical_government_stats.py); harvest APT-H-112 (Урок 9); cf. Whitney §303b
claims.note: RESOLVED from UNTESTABLE by the Classical-corpus instrument. The locative-of-feeling IS the leading enriched oblique for snih/abhilaṣ/anurañj, consistent with Whitney §303b and Apte — but the enrichment is weak (1.08x), because these verbs' locative competes with the ordinary locative-of-place and because the object of affection is often expressed by a genitive or a prepositional phrase. Graded OVERSTATED (direction right, strength overstated); the teaching target (locative) is defensible, hence pedagogy JUSTIFIED.
claims.methodichka: У глаголов любви местный падеж уместен, но не единственно возможен — направление верное, сила преувеличена (местный чувства конкурирует с обычным местным места).

claims.claim_ru: Все глаголы со значением движения управляют винительным падежом. [corroboration of APT-5]
claims.number: motion verbs (gam/yā/i/car/vraj/sṛ/dhāv/ā-gam) goal-case dependents: Acc 85.91% vs Dat+Loc 14.09% (n=873) — accusative is the heavy default, but the non-accusative goal is a real 14% minority (apte_treebank_stats.py D_motion_goal_case)
claims.ref: DCS-2021 treebank slice (apte_treebank_stats.py); harvest APT-H-15 (Урок 3); corroborates APT-5
claims.note: Puts a number on the APT-5 OVERSTATED flag: «все… винительным» is an 86%-true default, not an absolute — the dative and locative goal together account for ~14% (Loc conflates goal with place, so 14% is an upper bound; the dative goal alone is ~4.6%). For a composition primer the accusative default is the right teaching target, so pedagogy stays JUSTIFIED.
claims.methodichka: Тот же урок 3, но с точным числом: винительный цели — 85,9 %, неаккузативная цель (дательный + местный) — 14,1 %. Учите винительный как умолчание, а не как закон.

claims.claim_ru: Чаще всего частица kila означает воистину, за ней следует слово, которое эта частица усиливает.
claims.number: 0 of 22 tagged kila tokens are sentence-initial (apte_treebank_stats.py)
claims.note: Thin (n=22) but clean — postposition holds; consistent with the whole postpositive-particle family (ca/tu/ced/iva/eva/hi).

claims.claim_ru: Частица hi никогда не стоит в начале предложения.
claims.number: 7 of 904 tagged hi tokens are sentence-initial — 0.77% (apte_treebank_stats.py); hi was the method control for the particle-position battery, here promoted to its own claim
claims.note: Completes the postpositive-particle family (ca/tu/ced/iva/eva/kila/hi) — all confirmed never/rarely initial; hi at 0.77% matches eva (0.68%).

claims.claim_ru: Глагол ram отдыхать обычно является Ātmanepada, но в сочетании с приставками vi, ā или pari спрягается как Parasmaipada.
claims.number: finite present forms of ram: 95.5% Ātmanepada endings (276 Ā vs 13 P, n=289) — apte_pada_stats.py; DCS feat_voice tags only Pass, so pada was recovered from the -te/-ti ending
claims.ref: DCS-2021 present-ending pada proxy (apte_pada_stats.py); harvest APT-H-609 (Урок 29)
claims.note: Root-default voice cleanly confirmed. The preverb sub-rule (+vi/ā/pari → Parasmaipada) is a fine sense/preverb condition not separately drained (see APT-30).

claims.claim_ru: Корень han обычно спрягается в Parasmaipada; однако с предлогом ā спрягается в Ātmanepada, когда он непереходный.
claims.number: finite present forms of han: 89.9% Parasmaipada endings (1,315 P vs 147 A, n=1,462) — apte_pada_stats.py
claims.ref: DCS-2021 present-ending pada proxy (apte_pada_stats.py); harvest APT-H-612 (Урок 30)
claims.note: Root-default voice confirmed at large n; method-controlled (as/bhū → 100%/99.7% P, labh/īś → 97.6%/100% A).

claims.claim_ru: Корень krīḍ играть обычно спрягается в Parasmaipada, однако с приставками anu, saṃ, pari и ā — в Ātmanepada.
claims.number: finite present forms of krīḍ: 57.6% Parasmaipada / 42.4% Ātmanepada (87 P vs 64 A, n=151) — genuinely mixed, not Parasmaipada-dominant (apte_pada_stats.py)
claims.ref: DCS-2021 present-ending pada proxy (apte_pada_stats.py); harvest APT-H-602 (Урок 29)
claims.note: «Обычно Parasmaipada» overstates a near-even split — krīḍ uses both voices almost equally in the corpus, so «normally Parasmaipada» misleads on strength (the preverb condition Apte adds is real but the base claim itself is not the strong default it implies). Teaching a default pada is defensible, hence pedagogy JUSTIFIED.
claims.methodichka: Корневой залог по умолчанию учите как факт (ram → Ātmanepada ~95 %, han → Parasmaipada ~90 %, реестр APT-26/27), но krīḍ помните как «оба залога» — распределение почти поровну.

claims.claim_ru: Некоторые глагольные корни спрягаются только в одном залоге (nam, bhram, ruc, bhāṣ); некоторые — в обоих (kar, ci, cur, duh).
claims.number: single-voice confirmed: bhāṣ 97.9% Ātmanepada (n=282), nam 83.1% Parasmaipada (n=148); both-voice confirmed: kṛ 62% P / 38% Ā (n=6,095), duh 21% P / 79% Ā (n=243) — both padas substantially attested (apte_pada_stats.py)
claims.ref: DCS-2021 present-ending pada proxy (apte_pada_stats.py); harvest APT-H-600 (Урок 29)
claims.note: The single-voice/both-voice typology holds where drainable: bhāṣ and nam skew hard to one pada, kṛ and duh genuinely use both (minority pada 38% / 21%). ci/cur/ruc/bhram not separately measured here but the pattern is consistent.

claims.claim_ru: [домен] Тонкие правила залога по приставке и значению (kram+длительность → Ātm.; tap+ud, если непереходен → Ātm.; и т. п.).
claims.number: no signal path: DCS-2021 feat_voice tags only Passive (not Parasmaipada/Ātmanepada), and the ~15 fine rules (APT-H-601/603/604/605/606/607/608/610/611/613/614/615/617/618/619) condition on a SENSE ('when duration is implied', 'when intransitive', 'when referring to one's own body') that the corpus does not annotate
claims.ref: DCS-2021 feat_voice census + apte_pada_stats.py scope note; harvest APT-H-601..619 (Уроки 29–30)
claims.note: The honest floor of the voice domain: root-DEFAULT pada is recoverable from endings (APT-26..29), but the preverb+semantic conditions that make up most of lessons 29-30 are Pāṇinian sense-rules with no corpus signal — DCS neither tags the P/Ā distinction nor the semantic trigger. Closing instruments: a pada-tagged root lexicon plus sense-disambiguated preverb-verb tokens (neither exists in the sibling repos today). Documented, not force-verdicted.
claims.methodichka: Тонкие панинийские правила «приставка + значение» — справочный материал, а не то, что подтверждает частотность: корпус их не судит (DCS не размечает ни P/Ā, ни семантическое условие). Держите их как ориентир, не как проверенный закон.

claims.claim_ru: Аорист, помимо основного значения недавнего неопределенного прошлого, также выражает идею длительности действия. Имперфект не может быть использован в этом значении.
claims.number: Contradicted by Whitney §§927-929. §927: 'The aorist of the later language is simply a preterit, equivalent to the imperfect and perfect, and frequently coördinated with them' — so the imperfect is NOT barred from the sense the aorist carries; they are interchangeable in Classical Sanskrit. §928: the older-language aorist has the value of a 'perfect' — 'something past which is viewed as COMPLETED with reference to the present … what has just taken place' — i.e. completive/punctual, the opposite of durative 'continuousness'. §929: the aorist is distinguished FROM the imperfect precisely as a non-narrative tense. Whitney assigns the aorist no durative value anywhere.
claims.ref: Whitney 1889 §§927-929; Apte, Guide to Sanskrit Composition §210 (English original verified: 'also implies the idea of continuousness. The Imperfect cannot be used in this sense'); harvest APT-H-219 (Урок 19)
claims.note: The register's first FALSE. Two checks make it solid. (1) The error is APTE'S OWN, not the Likhushina translation's: the digitized English §210 reads 'also implies the idea of continuousness. The Imperfect cannot be used in this sense' — «длительность» faithfully renders 'continuousness', so the earlier translation-inversion hypothesis is refuted. (2) Apte contradicts himself: his §207-208 state that as Sanskrit ceased to be spoken 'writers began to use these three [past] tenses promiscuously' — exactly Whitney §927's equivalence — which undercuts §210's categorical 'the Imperfect cannot'. Apte is transmitting a Pāṇinian aspectual doctrine (the aorist as भूतार्थे लुङ्, the general past) as a categorical rule the descriptive record does not bear out — a prescriptive-vs-descriptive miss of the same family as APT-21 (the Pāṇinian dative-of-anger). Graded MISLEADING because a beginner told 'the aorist is durative, the imperfect is not' learns the aspect backwards; and the aorist is in any case marginal in Classical usage (~2.3% of finite verbal forms by DCS's feat_formation aorist tag — refreshed 17-07-2026 from the older 0.31% tense-code figure, which undercounted the root+thematic aorists, see whitney_aorist_tagger.py H1134; cf. Whitney §826), so the durative past the learner meets is overwhelmingly the imperfect/present, never the aorist.
claims.methodichka: Заучите наоборот: длительное, продолжающееся прошлое несут имперфект и презенс, а аорист — «завершительное, только что». Аорист в классике редок (~2,3 % глагольных форм в DCS по тегу формации аориста; Whitney §826), так что длительное прошлое вы почти всегда встретите имперфектом.

claims.claim_ru: Частица punaḥ обычно означает снова; но часто имеет значение тогда как, с другой стороны.
claims.number: systematic: Whitney §1112 (punar among the adverbial forms) + standard lexicon — punar = 'again, back; on the other hand, however'. Both senses Apte gives are standard.
claims.note: Both the iterative and the adversative sense are lexically standard; correctly reported.

claims.claim_ru: Частица prāyaḥ или prāyeṇa означает обычно, как правило, и используется для установки общего правила.
claims.number: systematic: Whitney §1111 lists 'prāyas usually' (accusative adverb) and §1112 c 'prāyeṇa mostly' (instrumental adverb) — Apte's gloss is verbatim Whitney.
claims.note: Directly attested in Whitney's own adverb lists, both the -as and -eṇa forms.

claims.claim_ru: muhuḥ означает часто … и в этом значении обычно повторяется.
claims.number: systematic: Whitney §1111 lists 'múhu and múhus' among the adverbs; the reduplicated muhur muhuḥ 'again and again' is the standard intensive.
claims.note: Adverb attested in Whitney; the reduplication for the iterative sense is standard.

claims.claim_ru: Наречие yataḥ означает откуда, когда используется вместо yasmāt.
claims.number: systematic: Whitney §1098 — the tas-adverbs (átas, tátas, yátas, kútas…) 'have an ablative sense, and not rarely also an ablative construction'; yátas = 'whence, from which' = the ablative yasmāt exactly.
claims.note: Precisely Whitney's account of the tas-adverbs — yatas is the ablative-sense adverb equivalent to yasmāt.

claims.claim_ru: yāvat, когда используется само по себе, означает до тех пор пока … Управляется винительным падежом.
claims.number: systematic: yāvat is the neuter of yā́vant (Whitney §510, a relative-root derivative); as a prepositional adverb 'up to, as far/long as' it takes the accusative of extent/limit (Whitney §1129, accusative with words expressing extent toward a limit).
claims.note: The accusative government of the extent-preposition yāvat is standard; correctly reported.

claims.claim_ru: Повторение yathā и tathā (yathā yathā — tathā tathā) используется с прилагательными в сравнительной степени.
claims.number: systematic: Whitney §1101 — yáthā and táthā are the manner-adverbs of the relative and demonstrative roots; the doubled yathā yathā … tathā tathā is the standard proportional correlative.
claims.note: Correlative manner-adverbs per Whitney; the proportional doubling is a standard idiom, correctly stated.

claims.claim_ru: hā чаще всего обозначает горе, сожаление, боль; значение отвращения (jugupsā) встречается крайне редко.
claims.number: systematic: Whitney §1135 a lists 'ā, hā, hāhā…' among the voice-gesture interjections; the grief/lament sense is the standard one, the disgust sense marginal — Apte's ranking is correct.
claims.note: hā classified as an interjection by Whitney; the grief-primary / disgust-rare split matches standard usage.

claims.claim_ru: Наречие varaṃ с na (за которыми, как правило, следует ca, tu или punaḥ) используется в значении лучше — чем; оба члена в именительном падеже.
claims.number: systematic: varam ('choice, better') + na is the standard classical preference idiom ('better X, [and] not Y'); both compared members stand in the nominative as predicate nouns, correlated by the adversative ca/tu/punar (Whitney §1133 b for tu, §1112 for punar).
claims.note: A correctly-described classical idiom; the nominative of both members and the adversative correlative are standard. Closes the substantive particle-sense set; the residual lesson-26-28 candidates (balavat adverbial, yat/yena clause-linkers) are uncontested descriptive glosses left in the harvest.
